hazard.summary: An explicit output delivery contract audit independently surfaced the same execution-reliability problems seen in other systems: unbounded retry creates liveness risk, delivery outcomes collapse too early at the checkpoint seam, and control-flow behavior still defines correctness where contract semantics should.
signals: the only circuit-breaker is the 60s per-batch timeout
Rejected advances checkpoint = data loss by design
worker panic can bypass ack path
delivery outcomes collapse too early at the pipeline checkpoint seam
fanout behavior is defined and tested as a contract
retry behavior is bounded, observable, and policy-driven (desired end-state)

constraints: Retryable failure paths must have an explicit terminal boundary.
Delivery outcomes must remain explicit across the worker-to-checkpoint seam rather than collapsing into incidental control flow.
Checkpoint advancement rules must follow delivery contract semantics, not implementation accident.
Fanout aggregation precedence and retry behavior must be explicit, bounded, and testable.
verification: Verify retryable sink failures terminate after a bounded retry budget instead of looping forever.
Verify worker panic paths still emit failure outcomes and do not strand tickets.
Verify rejected outcomes and retry-exhausted outcomes produce distinct checkpoint behavior.
Verify fanout aggregation semantics remain deterministic under mixed sink results.
notes: This receipt is valuable primarily as external convergence. A team working on a log-forwarding delivery contract independently surfaced the same class of execution-reliability problems documented elsewhere: retryable branches without terminal boundaries become liveness failures, and correctness degrades when outcome semantics collapse too early. This extends the corpus beyond AI-specific systems and shows the pattern is general to execution infrastructure.

constraints: preserve typed outcomes across checkpoint seams
anti_patterns: retry forever until timeout becomes the only boundary
checkpoint progression coupled to collapsed delivery outcomes
correctness determined by incidental control flow
